title: BWC & Incident Records: Roland Caballero Shootout — Three HPD Officers Shot, Vehicle Pursuit & SWAT Standoff – Houston, TX (January 2022)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the shooting of three Houston Police Department officers by suspect Roland Caballero, the subsequent vehicle pursuit, carjacking, and SWAT standoff.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: January 27, 2022
Location: Initial shooting at McGowen Street and Hutchins Street (Third Ward area); SWAT standoff at 1800 block of Lockwood Drive (Fifth Ward), Houston, Texas
Suspect Name: Roland Caballero, 31, of Houston
Involved Officers: Officers Hayden, Gadson, Alvarez, Ricinos-Diaz, and additional SWAT officers
 
Incident Summary: Officers responded to a threat-to-life call and pursued Caballero's Dodge Charger. After crashing near McGowen and Hutchins streets, Caballero exited and opened fire with a fully automatic weapon, striking three officers. He then carjacked a white Mercedes and fled. Officers later located the vehicle at his Lockwood Drive residence, where a SWAT standoff ensued with additional shots fired. Caballero surrendered after approximately five hours. He was charged with three counts of attempted capital murder of a police officer, aggravated robbery, and federal weapons charges.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Officers Hayden, Gadson, Alvarez, Ricinos-Diaz, and all other HPD officers who were involved in the initial traffic stop, vehicle pursuit, and shootout near McGowen and Hutchins streets.
 
All BWC footage from SWAT officers who responded to and engaged the suspect during the standoff at his Lockwood Drive residence.
 
All dashboard camera (dashcam) footage from all HPD patrol vehicles involved in the pursuit and present at both the initial shooting scene and the standoff location.
 
Any surveillance camera footage recovered by investigators from the area of the initial crash and shootout.
 
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives, supplemental reports, and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD (Computer-Aided Dispatch) logs related to the initial threat-to-life call, the pursuit, the officer-down calls, and the SWAT standoff.
 
Any booking or intake video/audio recordings following Caballero's surrender and transport to the hospital.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrej Ilic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Juan Ayon-Barraza Kidnapping Pursuit & Fatal Officer-Involved Shooting – Richmond, CA (April 2020)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the fatal officer-involved shooting of kidnapping suspect Juan Ayon-Barraza following a high-speed multi-agency pursuit that ended in Richmond.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: April 16, 2020
Location: South 37th Street and Stege Avenue / Carlson Boulevard, Richmond, California
Suspect Name: Juan Carlos Ayon-Barraza, 24, of Vacaville/Vallejo
Victim: Tanisha Thomas, 29, of Vallejo (kidnapping victim)
Involved Officers: Richmond PD Officer Deorian; Oakland PD Officers Recendez and Hohn
Agencies Involved: Richmond Police Department, Oakland Police Department, Moraga Police Department, Contra Costa County Sheriff's Office, California Highway Patrol
 
Incident Summary: On the morning of April 16, 2020, Vallejo Police received a missing person report for 29-year-old Tanisha Thomas, who was last seen with Ayon-Barraza. That evening at approximately 6:45 p.m., a cyclist called 911 reporting a man in a white Chevrolet Astro van dumping what appeared to be a body on the side of the road near Pinehurst Road and Skyline Boulevard in unincorporated Contra Costa County. Responding officers from Oakland, Moraga, and the Contra Costa County Sheriff's Office located the victim, who had been shot in the head and severely beaten. The suspect's van was spotted and a pursuit ensued, reaching speeds over 100 mph on Interstate 580 and Highway 13. The 20-minute chase ended in Richmond at South 37th Street and Stege Avenue, where Ayon-Barraza made a U-turn and rammed his van into Oakland police vehicles, injuring two officers. Officers from Richmond and Oakland fired on the suspect after he pointed a firearm in their direction. Ayon-Barraza sustained a fatal gunshot wound and was pronounced dead at the scene. Thomas died approximately one month later from her injuries.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Richmond Police Officer Deorian and any other Richmond PD officers who responded to or were involved in the pursuit and shooting.
 
All BWC footage from Oakland Police Officers Recendez and Hohn and any other Oakland PD officers present during the pursuit and final confrontation.
 
All dashboard camera (dashcam) footage from all Richmond PD and Oakland PD patrol vehicles involved in the pursuit and at the shooting scene.
 
All helicopter or aerial camera footage captured during the pursuit.
 
The complete incident report from the Richmond Police Department, including all officer narratives and supplemental reports.
 
All 911 call recordings related to this incident, including the initial call from the cyclist reporting the body being dumped and any calls from the pursuit and shooting scene.
 
All dispatch/CAD (Computer-Aided Dispatch) logs related to the pursuit and officer-involved shooting.
 
Any use-of-force reports filed by officers who discharged their weapons.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrej Ilic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Stephanie Bloodworth DUI Pursuit – Duck Key, FL (February 2022)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.

I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the DUI arrest of Stephanie Marie Bloodworth following a vehicle pursuit on Duck Key.

Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: February 21, 2022
Location: North Bahama Drive, Duck Key, Monroe County, Florida
Suspect Name: Stephanie Marie Bloodworth (DOB: 11/01/1997)
Arresting Agency: Monroe County Sheriff's Office
Arresting Deputy: Deputy Connor O'Neill

Incident Summary: Multiple deputies responded to Duck Key at approximately 6:30 p.m. after a caller reported a gray Tesla nearly hit a tree and pedestrians. Deputy Connor O'Neill spotted the Tesla parked on North Bahama Drive and the driver matched the caller's description. The driver, later identified as Bloodworth, put the car in drive and sped away as Deputy O'Neill approached. A vehicle pursuit ensued through Duck Key streets with Bloodworth running stop signs at a high rate of speed. Bloodworth stopped briefly, pushed Deputy O'Neill away, and fled a second time before being stopped and detained. She resisted arrest, kicked multiple deputies, and was ultimately taken into custody. Breathalyzer results showed a blood alcohol level of .251, over three times the legal limit. She was charged with fleeing and eluding, DUI, resisting arrest, and reckless driving.

Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Deputy Connor O'Neill and all other responding deputies involved in the initial contact, vehicle pursuit, arrest, and transport of Stephanie Bloodworth.
 
All dashboard camera (dashcam) and in-car camera footage from all responding patrol vehicles present during the pursuit, arrest, and transport.
 
Any rear-seat camera footage from the patrol vehicle in which Bloodworth was transported.
 
The complete incident and arrest report, including all officer narratives and supplemental reports.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD (Computer-Aided Dispatch) logs related to the initial reckless driving report and subsequent pursuit.
 
Any booking or intake video/audio recordings, including sobriety evaluation footage from the Monroe County Detention Center.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Andrej Ilic

title: REQUEST FOR IN CUSTODY DEATH DATA

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: REQUEST FOR IN CUSTODY DEATH DATA
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting all in custody death data for the Washoe County Detention Facility from 01/01/2000 to present to include date of death, name, age, race, gender, cause of death, manner of death, coronoer report results.  I was previously provided a spreadsheet in 2023 from the WCSO that contained this requested data. I would like the updated spreadsheet that contains in custody deaths to present date

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Annemarie Grant
